text.title: Cron tick semantics: heartbeat, not control flow
text.summary: A `*/5 * * * *` cron tick wakes the orchestrator if it has gone idle. The tick is a heartbeat (drain detection, post-compaction recovery) — NOT control flow. The orchestrator drives iterations continuously without waiting for the next tick whenever there is work to do.

section.title: Tick action sequence
section.p: On every tick, the orchestrator runs this sequence in order.

list.item: Identify the current phase and the set of pending tasks for it.
If a background subagent is still running for the current phase, no-op and append a one-line "still in flight" entry to the log.
If every output file the current phase declares exists on disk, advance phase: update `state.md`, append the transition to the decision log, dispatch the next-phase subagent set.
If a subagent failed (no output file within the expected window), re-dispatch with the same brief. After two failures on the same task, fall back to the failure-modes recovery path.
Append a single line to the `state.md` log — symbols, not paragraphs.

rule.statement: The cron tick is a keepalive signal. The orchestrator MUST drive the next iteration immediately whenever it has work to do — it does NOT wait for the next tick between dispatches.
rule.rationale: Waiting for cron between iterations adds 5 minutes of idle per step and turns a one-day project into a multi-week loop. Cron exists to recover from drain (no in-flight subagents) and from context compaction (orchestrator state lost). Steady-state throughput comes from the orchestrator's own continuous loop.

rule.statement: The orchestrator MUST be fully restartable from `.product/state.md` plus the on-disk file layout. After context compaction, a fresh orchestrator reads `state.md`, identifies the current phase, and resumes — it does NOT require any in-memory state from the previous session.
rule.rationale: Context compaction is inevitable on multi-day projects. If the orchestrator depends on in-memory state, every compaction is a reset. Persisting all state to `state.md` and the file layout makes the loop survive compaction transparently.

section.title: One-line log format
section.p: Every tick adds exactly one line to the log section of `state.md`. Lines use symbols and short fragments — never paragraphs. Detail goes to commits or to the open-questions section.

example.code: 2026-05-07T14:05Z phase=1 dispatched=3 inflight=3 ✓ 2026-05-07T14:10Z phase=1 inflight=2 done=1 ✓ 2026-05-07T14:15Z phase=1 done → phase=2 dispatched=1 ✓ 2026-05-07T14:20Z phase=2 inflight=1 ✓
example.note: Symbols carry the verdict. The full detail of what each subagent produced is on disk in `research/`, not in the log.

antipattern.description: Multi-paragraph tick entries narrating the orchestrator's reasoning, the subagent's output, and the next intended action.
antipattern.reason: The log balloons over a multi-day run, future ticks must scan it to find current state, and the same content already lives in `research/` and `decisions/`.
